extra.reasoning:
The property NOT(rec_drug_a AND rec_drug_b) holds across all 4 input combinations.

Only rec_drug_a and rec_drug_b matter for the property. Their values:

- rec_drug_a: set true only by r3 (priority 50), which requires diabetes=true. Otherwise default false.
- rec_drug_b: r1 (priority 90, sets false) and r2 (priority 50, sets true) both require diabetes=true. When diabetes=true, both match; highest priority wins, so r1 (priority 90) beats r2 (priority 50) and rec_drug_b := false. When diabetes=false, neither matches, default false.

Case diabetes=false (prior_gi_bleed either value): rec_drug_a=false, rec_drug_b=false → not both true.

Case diabetes=true (prior_gi_bleed either value): rec_drug_a=true (r3), rec_drug_b=false (r1 at priority 90 overrides r2) → not both true.

The key interaction is that the higher-priority r1 always suppresses rec_drug_b under diabetes, so rec_drug_a and rec_drug_b are never jointly true. Property holds for the entire input space; no counterexample exists.
